description: Embrace the challenges of 2020 and Covid19 with a minimalist, geometric email template that stylishly encapsulates the spirit of learning and adaptation. This design, seamlessly blending into the Education and Others sectors, offers a platform to share insights and knowledge about navigating the school environment amidst the pandemic. The layout is ideal for sharing video content, fostering a sense of community and connection while maintaining safety protocols. Its minimalist design ensures a clear, uncluttered message, while the geometric elements add a modern, dynamic touch. This email template is the perfect tool for communicating effectively in our constantly evolving world.
short_description: Navigate the challenges of 2020 and Covid19 with a minimalist, geometric email template. Designed for the Education sector, it's perfect for sharing video content and learning resources. Its clean design ensures clear messaging, while geometric elements add a modern touch. Ideal for communicating in our evolving world.

designer.description: I have a degree in graphic design and I am dedicated to the world of communication providing graphic solutions to companies to get the best out of them. I consider myself a detail-oriented, demanding and multidisciplinary person, passionate about the world of communication. I believe in design as a powerful tool to communicate values, ideas and emotions, this is what I try to transfer in any project to always get the best result.

html_data:
School Learners COVID-19 Support
 

+ COVID-19

Adapting

to the new

normal

Visit website
 

Due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic

There has been a great deal of disruption to learning, the impact of which will be felt long into the next academic year and possibly beyond.

Read more
 

Learning has moved from public to personal spaces

The most obvious change has been learning shifting from the classroom to the home, with technology needing to be adopted and improved to accommodate this.

Read more

Due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic
There has been a great deal of disruption to learning, the impact of which will be felt long into the next academic year and possibly beyond.
Learning has moved from public to personal spaces
The most obvious change has been learning shifting from the classroom to the home, with technology needing to be adopted and improved to accommodate this.
